stimulus:
The following text is adapted from an 1894 poem by a fictional author. The speaker describes a winter painting in a gallery.

Beneath the bare and frost-touched trees,

   No bird, no song, no laughter near,

Nor guarding wall is seen—

   Though winds blow sharp and clear.
stem: As used in the text, what does the word “guarding” most nearly mean?
rationale: Choice B is correct because, in context, a wall that guards offers protection from the sharp winter winds. "Protective" matches this sense. Choice A is incorrect because the wall would shield, not decorate. Choice C is incorrect because affection has nothing to do with the cold setting. Choice D is incorrect because there is no suggestion of suspicion in the text.
